sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see something important! There are TWO blue squares near the left frame:
1. [[29, 17], [29, 18], [30, 18], [30, 17]] - inside the left frame at row 28-31
2. [[33, 17], [33, 18], [34, 18], [34, 17]] - a NEW blue square below the left frame
And the player's W part is now a 2x2 square at [[36, 16], [36, 19]] (only 4 pixels).
Looking at the image, I can see the left frame now has TWO blue squares inside it - one at the top and one below. This is different from before when there was only one blue square inside each frame.
Wait, let me reconsider. The blue square at [[29, 17], ...] is inside the left frame (row 28-31). The blue square at [[33, 17], ...] is below the left frame (row 33-34). 
So pressing SPACE created a new blue square below the left frame! This might be the key mechanic: SPACE creates a blue square "token" at the player's current position (or near it), which can then be used to collect frames.
Looking at the image more carefully, I see:
- Left frame (charcoal) at row 28-31 with a blue square inside
- A new blue square at row 33-34 (below the left frame)
- Player's W part is now a 2x2 at row 36
- Player's N part is at row 37-39
So SPACE seems to:
1. Shrink the player's W part from 4x4 to 2x2
2. Create a blue square token near the player
Maybe the blue square token is what collects frames? Let me think about this...
Actually, looking at the right frame collection again. At step 20, the player was at col 44-47, and the right frame was at col 44-47. When the player pressed UP, the player moved and the frame was collected. But maybe a blue square token was created earlier and that's what collected the frame.
Let me check the history to see when blue square tokens appear.
Actually, I notice there's now a blue square at [[33, 17], ...] which is between the player and the left frame. Maybe this blue square needs to move into the frame to collect it. Let me try pressing UP to move the blue square (or player) towards the frame.
But first, let me understand the current state better. The player's W part is at [[36, 16], [36, 19]], which is a 2x2 square. The N part is at [[37, 16], [37, 19], [39, 19], [39, 16]], which is a 3x3 shape. The blue square token is at [[33, 17], [33, 18], [34, 18], [34, 17]].
Wait, the blue square at [[33, 17], ...] is at row 33-34, col 17-18. The left frame is at row 28-31, col 16-19. The blue square is just below the left frame.
Let me try pressing UP to see if the blue square moves into the frame and collects it.

<parameter=code>

# Let me try pressing UP to move the token into the frame
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
